title: Burr Coffee Grinder, Electric Conical Coffee Bean Grinder With 30 Adjustable Grind Settings For 2-12 Cups, Sliver & Black
description:
About this item
【Durable Conical Burr】With 40-millimeter stainless steel conical burrs, OLLYGRIN burr coffee grinder ensure optimal flavor extraction and create uniform grounds.
【Precise Grinding Settings】The OLLYGRIN burr coffee grinder offers 30 precise grind settings to make everything from an ultra-fine grind for espresso to a coarser grind for french press coffee for your customized brewing method.
【Keep the Optimal Flavor of Coffee】 OLLYGRIN burr coffee grinder has a gear reduction motor, which generates the slow grinding action. The coffee grinder produces less heat from friction and ensures the flavor and aroma of the coffee beans intact.
【Large Capacity & Auto-locking System】 OLLYGRIN burr coffee grinder has a large hopper, it is capable of holding 10 ounces of whole beans. Also allows to store enough beans for several servings of coffee. Ollygrin coffee grinder has a auto-locking system, the coffee hopper on burr grinder can resist the beans from spilling out for easy removal, storage, and transfer of the bean hopper.
【Double Safety Protection】* Friction clutch prevents damage to the grinding gear of burr grinder in the event of small stones in your beans. **Built-in overheating protection protects the motor of burr grinder to ensure the coffee grinder a long life.
【Simple Operation & Easy to Clean】The unique rotary button two-in-one, one-button design in coffee grinder makes the grinding process very simple and easy to operate. The adjustable 2-12 cup settings of coffee grinder is good for single cup for one or a large gathering. The upper burr lift-off option and included brush allows you to clean the burr coffee grinder with ease.
reviews_5s:
This burr grinder does an excellent job. It's quiet and quick. It's good-looking and easy to use. Well worth every penny.I love this grinder. I had a bodum for years that had a non-skid type surface all over the unit, which means everything stuck to it and it never felt clean. Not so with this grinder. I love how easy it is to clean and there's no static! I love the settings and it's very easy to use. The price is right. I like the design, so it sits on my countertop, unlike my old bodum. It's quite quiet and quick. Makes a great tasting cappuccino in my little moka pot.This coffee grinder is quieter than the one we had before, it's easier to use because of several features. There is a lock on the top in case you want to remove the container that holds the coffee, the receptacle that receives the coffee does not have to be lined up in anyway because it's round and it just snaps perfectly in the place. You can select the number of cups you want to brew, and you have a full range of grinding choices. We just set these two choices one time, and because we loved the way the coffee came out. We just leave it like it is and in the morning, we push one button to grind a fresh amount of coffee for the day. It's our favorite coffee grinder we've had.Waited to use this unit for awhile to make sure my review was accurate. Works great and very powerful to produce variable grind. Like that the grind holder is directly beneath the grinder wheel, causes less mess!
The dial is a little hard to read, but seems to work well. I have to set it a little more to get the amount of grinds perfect.A good design! It looks good on my counter- just the right size. Whole beans in the top cup which has a dial below where you can fine tune the grind size. Below is the on switch with its dial for choosing how much coffee gets ground. Every morning press the grind button and get perfect amount of ground coffee. Maybe the best part : the grinding is not loud!First I read a great deal of reviews on this particular model before I purchased it. I am going for an automatic drip coffee maker to a French Press which is what lead me to this model. My personal opinions on this unit so far.

1) It is slightly noisy, but not so much that you cannot be in the room with it while it is being used. Has not scared anyone in the house yet, including the dogs, so definitely not a deal breaker as some had mentioned.

2) Ease of use could not get any simpler. Set for the desired grind, course for the French press, and the amount of grind for the amount of coffee you are making. These are subjective to the user, but the unit is very straight forward to use.

3) Messy or have to clean up the unit. I am not having the issue that some have stated or shown pictures of. If the lid is on the grind container and it is pushed all the way back I have not had any grinds other than those in the container. Again I am user the course setting for a French Press so they may explain why I am not seeing what others may be experiencing.

Bottom line for me, functions as it is described, super easy to use, and paired with a good French Press makes a Great cup of coffee.This grinder has been wonderful. Easy to operate, and clean. We love that we can change the settings for more coarse or fine grind depending on what we want that day, also can set how many cups of beans need to be ground. Push button to start grinding and finished in few seconds.
Great little grinder for the fresh ground taste that doesn't break the bank.Just received it and after cleaning gave it a run through. It's great because it's very quiet and the hopper holds a lot of beans for grinding. If it works this well over the long haul then I'd say it's definitely worth the extra cost vs the cheaper grinders. It was actually larger than I expected but that's mainly due to the large bean holder.Originally purchased a Cuisinart grinder in the same price range from Target. Emptying the plastic container, after it ground the beans, was a nightmare. Static cling made the process a serious mess. Returned it! This one is way quieter, and very easy to dump the grounds without “dusting” the pantry. We like the grounds very fine, and it does a great job. The small footprint makes it pretty invisible to leave on our coffee stationWe needed a replacement for our cuisinart burr grinder. So glad we took a chance here! We prefer this awesome little machine! It’s simple and easy to use and way less mess than our original.
reviews_1s:
UPDATE after 6-months:
Well DANG if this thing didn't fool me for a good while! I was SO thrilled about it. Then after about 4-months it started with the 'static' issue. Not a biggie at first - and I loved that I could remove most of the burr to clean it & it seemed to really help the static when it was nice & clean.....TEMPORARILY.
Then another couple of months and this thing is SPRAYING grinds everywhere when I remove the catch bin. Additionally, the catch bin is not longer 'catching' all of the grounds! Each grind I'm removing about a tablespoon of grinds from under & behind the catch bin. I even resorted to grinding the night before & letting it sit til morning (NOT OPTIMAL as the grinds aren't really fresh) and it helps SOME, but still lots of static - more than any grinder I've ever used!!! CRAZY!!

PREVIOUS REVIEW:
I was trepidatious about ditching my old cuisinart grinder because it do espresso grind so well. But alas after about 4 years it began to fail.
One of the things I decided to look for when finding a new grinder was the location of the catch-bin, as a downside of the cuisinart was the catch bin 'entry point' was on the backside - where as the hopper/grinder is above it and it created a constant need for cleaning (grinds would get caught constantly) so I wanted to find a grinder where the entry to the catch bin was 'directly into it' from above/from the grinder. "Engineering-ly" this just seems more efficient - and it is!!

This grinder has not only been much quieter (all grinders are going to make some noise, but this is a drastic improvement on noise level); additionally, the grind quality is fantastic! Weather grinding course or this grinder does an amazing job.

The theory of the location of the catch bin in relation to the grinder has been a life changer, not only is the grind better, but nothing ever seems to get caught AND it's so much faster. I'm sure eventually it will need cleaning, but this grinder has a lift away segment of the hopper so you can get into the burr area and clean everything… revolutionary! ONLY WISH..... would be a stainless catch container.

Static ...... for the first 4-6 months (can't recall exactly) there was NO static during grinding. Eventually with buildup on the burrs it started to be the normal static issue. I do love you can remove the top-burr & hopper to clean it - using an air-can & brush to clean the lower hopper. It helps, but static does return. Helps to wet a spoon & stir beans before grinding. Would be better yet if it had a stainless catch-bin.

Absolutely amazing purchase and so very happy with this grinder! Even if by some chance, it doesn't last as long as my cuisinart did… I think I would rather buy another one of these every year - due to the efficiency of the grind, the quietness, and the (initial) lack of static! One of my best purchases on Amazon!UPDATE: Grinder seized up and is nonfunctional after just 8 grinding sessions.
We received the Ollygrin unit yesterday to replace the Cuisenart mill that we used for 2.5 years...the motor gave out. After a search of reviews, etc. of burr coffee mills, I decided on this Ollygrin coffee mill based on design, features and cost. I am VERY PLEASED with this choice based on my initial use:
1) The stainless mill components ground the 2.5 lbs. of coffee effortlessly and in half the time of our old mill. The mill was relatively easy to clean upon completion of the grind.
2) The Ollygrin mill was very noticeably more quiet during the grinding process.
3) In comparison, it's a very clean unit to use. Statically debris was very minimal and the 12 cup grind container was well designed to avoid debris escaping during operation.
4) We like a French Roast which is characteristically a bit oily... unlike the Cuisenart, coffee fed easily and consistently from the hopper into the mill without "clogging".
5) The slightly longer cord on the Ollygrin is a nice feature. It is much easier to position the unit on the counter during operation.
So far, LOTS of positives! IF ONLY THEY HAD LASTED!At first I loved this grinder but after about a month I realized that it makes an incredible mess after each use. Coffee grinds fly out of the machine and it is just too messy to use.Had high hopes it would work like an OXO grinder, which it closely resembles, but no such luck. Broken Christmas present.They replaced the first one due to the coffee grinding more and more slowly and finer over time causing the coffee grounds to get very warm. it also appeared to be out of balance (wobbled). The second one did not wobble but eventually did the same thing. Yes , I took it apart and cleaned it many times. For some reason the coffee beans sit in the grinding section and will not move to the container after grinding, fast enough, even after blowing it out with a compressor. Of course, after replacing it a couple of months ago I am not eligible for a refund.I ordered Ollygrin Coffee grinder on May 21,2024, less than 6 months ago and it stopped working! We woke up this morning and for no apparent reason it just stopped working! Never purchase anything from this company! Horrible product! Buyer be wear! Amazon doesn’t back up their products either! Quit purchasing from Amazon too!
J. SmithOrdered on Sept 21,2024
Stopped working on Dec 2,2024
Only used once a week, so got about 8 uses out of it before it diedThe grinder started making a clicking noise and does not grind coffee beans.The beans got stuck in the grinder each time. I had to take apart and remove the beans from between the gears. I am returning.After grinding coffee beans the container & lid are full of -dust-like particles that create a mess when poured into Espresso; or French Press or pour over.
I DO not recommend it!
